description: This satellite image, captured by SPOT-5 at 2.5 m resolution on 2011-02-24 using a 1/2/3 band combination (false colour infrared), shows the permanent dead side-arms of the Danube river near Tolna, Hungary. The main Danube river channel runs through the image, appearing dark blue/black, with an elongated island visible within it. To the west of the main channel, a large, dark blue/black, meandering water body represents an oxbow lake or dead side-arm, indicating a former river course. Surrounding these water features are extensive agricultural fields, appearing in vibrant red and pink tones due to the false colour representation of healthy vegetation. Patches of light grey and finely textured areas suggest urban or built-up land. The image source is CNES 2011©, Distribution Airbus DS/Spot Image.
